title: Student Essay Contest to Celebrate America250, Diamond Says
raw_text:
Student Essay Contest to Celebrate America250, Diamond Says
May 8, 2026
JONESTOWN â A newly created student essay contest invites Pennsylvania high school students to participate in a historic statewide initiative commemorating the 250th anniversary of the United States, according to Rep. Russ Diamond (R-Lebanon).
A component of the America250 celebrations includes a ceremonial session of the Pennsylvania House of Representatives on Wednesday, Sept. 9, at Congress Hall in Philadelphia. Each House district will select one student to represent their community and take part in the event, which aims to highlight the legislative branchâs role and encourage youth participation in government.
âThis anniversary offers a valuable moment for students to consider our nationâs founding ideals and how they continue to shape our lives today,â Diamond said. âI encourage students to take part, share their viewpoints, and engage in this meaningful civic opportunity.â
Rising high school juniors or seniors who reside in the 102nd District are eligible to enter by submitting an essay about the countryâs founding principles. The deadline to apply is June 1.
Educators and school administrators are encouraged to share the contest with eligible students and support their participation.
Municipalities in the 102nd District include Annville, Bethel, East Hanover, Heidelberg, Jackson, Millcreek, North Annville, North Londonderry, Swatara and Union townships as well as Cleona, Jonestown, Myerstown, Palmyra and Richland boroughs.
Submit essays by emailing rmiccarelli@pahousegop.com. Call 717-277-2101 with questions.
Representative Russ Diamond
102nd  District
Pennsylvania House of Representatives
Media Contact: Jennifer Fitch
717.260.6563
jfitch@pahousegop.com
RepDiamond.com

title: Diamond to Offer Pop-Up Offices
raw_text:
Diamond to Offer Pop-Up Offices
February 24, 2026
JONESTOWN â To better serve constituents closer to their homes and businesses, Rep. Russ Diamond (R-Lebanon) will start offering âpop-up officesâ for staff to provide assistance with state-related matters.
On the first Wednesday of every month, staff will be available to help constituents from 9 a.m. to noon at The Caring Cupboard, 131 N. Railroad St., Palmyra.
On the third Wednesday of every month, services will be provided from 9 a.m. to noon at the Myerstown Borough Building at 101 S. Railroad St.
âWeâll be able to provide a full range of services at these locations, so they will be a great place to bring vehicle registration problems, birth certificate requests and similar issues,â Diamond said. âMy hope is these offices will be a nice complement to our full-time office at 175 S. Lancaster St. in Jonestown.â
Appointments are encouraged for the pop-up offices, but walk-ins are welcome. Call 717-277-2101 for more information.
Representative Russ Diamond

Pennsylvania House of Representatives
